title: Run Go quality gates in continuous integration
description:
## What to build

Make CI enforce the repository quality gates that are currently documented in the Makefile: build, vet, tests, lint, race tests where supported, and generated-skill synchronization.

Keep smoke tests that require external infrastructure as a separate explicitly labeled job.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CI runs the supported Go toolchain and a compile or build check.
- [ ] CI runs `go vet ./...` and the unit/system test suite.
- [ ] CI runs lint and generated-skill drift checks.
- [ ] Failures include actionable logs and do not depend on a developer-local cache.
- [ ] External smoke tests are clearly separated from deterministic checks.
- [ ] The CI contract is documented and the current host-sensitive error assertion is made portable.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: CI runs deterministic build, vet, unit/system tests, lint, race tests where supported, and generated-skill synchronization under the supported Go toolchain. External smoke tests remain a separate explicitly labeled job.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Preserve bounded memory in large-file imports
description:
## What to build

Remove unconditional whole-file reads from the enhanced import path so hashing, type detection, parsing, and storage can operate within the configured streaming memory budget.

Preserve the existing content-addressed identity for identical input bytes.

## Evidence

The enhanced importer reads the entire input with `os.ReadFile` before invoking streaming analysis. A large file can therefore exceed memory before the streaming parser and memory limit take effect.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Generate a file substantially larger than the configured streaming memory budget.
2. Import it through the enhanced importer.
3. Observe process memory grow with the full file size before bounded parsing completes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Large JSON, NDJSON, YAML, and CSV imports do not require a full input-sized byte slice.
- [ ] The configured memory budget remains meaningful under large-file tests.
- [ ] Content hashes remain identical to the existing raw-byte hash contract.
- [ ] Imports remain cancellable and report useful errors on resource exhaustion.
- [ ] Regression tests exercise files substantially larger than the configured budget.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: preserve the existing raw-byte content hash while removing unconditional whole-file buffering. Hashing and parsing should be streamed or otherwise bounded; the configured memory budget must remain meaningful for all supported large-file formats.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Enforce --only scope during model convergence
description:
## What to build

Make `pudl run MODEL --converge --only RESOURCE` constrain the actual reconciliation scope, not just the displayed plan. The selected resource set must be applied consistently when generating manifests, invoking mu, recording lifecycle status, and producing the final report.

Unknown resource names should fail before any side effect.

## Evidence

The current CLI parses and prints `--only`, but `runConvergeLoop` receives the unfiltered model and writes every desired manifest.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a model with at least two desired resources.
2. Run `pudl run MODEL --converge --only RESOURCE_A`.
3. Inspect the generated manifests, mu operations, or lifecycle report.
4. Observe that the unselected resource is still included.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Only selected resources are rendered, applied, and promoted during convergence.
- [ ] Unselected resources are not mutated or reported as newly converged.
- [ ] Unknown selectors fail before reconciliation starts.
- [ ] Tests cover single-resource selection, multiple selectors, and no selector.
- [ ] The chosen dependency-closure and reporting semantics are documented.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: `--only` applies to `pudl run MODEL --converge --only RESOURCE`. The effective set is the selected resources plus required declared dependencies. Unknown selectors fail before side effects. Unselected resources remain untouched and are reported as untouched rather than newly converged.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Refresh architecture and testing documentation to match the implementation
description:
## What to build

Update architecture and testing documentation so it describes the current catalog schema, dependency versions, package layout, import behavior, and available test suites.

Retain historical material only where it is clearly marked as historical.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Architecture docs use current catalog terminology and fields.
- [ ] Version claims match `go.mod` and the supported toolchain.
- [ ] Testing docs reference only packages and commands that exist.
- [ ] Streaming, envelope, inventory, workspace, and convergence behavior match the implementation.
- [ ] Known gaps and skipped tests are listed with owners or linked tickets.
- [ ] Documentation links and examples are checked.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: documentation describes implemented behavior and marks historical material explicitly. It must include the selected convergence, inventory, envelope, collection, and workspace contracts plus known skipped coverage.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Unify envelope and schema metadata processing across import modes
description:
## What to build

Route single-file, batch, and stdin imports through one envelope-aware metadata pipeline. Envelope unwrapping, schema-reference recording, item-schema persistence, and schema-cache updates must have the same contract in every input mode.

Metadata failures must follow an explicit error policy instead of being hidden by default.

## Evidence

The single-file path extracts envelopes and records schema metadata, while batch and stdin paths call the importer directly. The single-file metadata-recording error is also suppressed unless debug logging is enabled.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Prepare the same supported envelope input.
2. Import it once as a regular file, once through batch or wildcard input, and once through stdin.
3. Compare stored payloads, schema references, item schemas, and schema-cache entries.
4. Observe that the paths do not necessarily produce equivalent metadata.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Regular file, wildcard or batch, and stdin imports all unwrap supported envelopes consistently.
- [ ] Declared and inferred schema metadata is recorded consistently in all modes.
- [ ] Malformed or ambiguous envelopes have documented behavior and tests.
- [ ] Metadata persistence failures are visible to callers according to the chosen policy.
- [ ] End-to-end tests verify equivalent results across all input modes.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: consolidate regular-file, batch, and stdin imports behind one envelope-aware pipeline. Supported envelopes unwrap identically in every mode. Malformed or ambiguous envelopes fail the import, and schema metadata/cache persistence failures are surfaced rather than silently downgraded.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Preserve collection membership when item content is deduplicated
description:
## What to build

Allow the same content-addressed item to belong to multiple collections without silently dropping later collection membership.

The catalog model and list/query APIs should make the relationship explicit.

## Evidence

Item deduplication is global by content hash, while a catalog item row has one collection association. Repeated content imported into another collection can therefore disappear from collection-scoped listings.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create two collections containing the same item bytes.
2. Import both collections.
3. List items for each collection.
4. Observe that the content-addressed item is not necessarily visible in both collections.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Importing identical content into two collections preserves both memberships.
- [ ] Collection-scoped list and query operations return every member.
- [ ] Global content-addressed identity remains stable and deduplicated.
- [ ] Removing or reimporting one collection does not corrupt another membership.
- [ ] The relationship model and migration strategy are documented and tested.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: retain global content-addressed item identity but model collection membership as many-to-many. Importing or removing one collection must not affect another collection that references the same item.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Prevent partial-success collection imports
description:
## What to build

Define and enforce collection import atomicity. An item failure must not leave a collection that reports success while its declared item count and stored item rows disagree.

Choose either all-or-nothing import or an explicit resumable partial state, and expose that state through the CLI and catalog.

## Evidence

Collection item creation prints warnings and continues after item errors, then returns success. The collection record count can therefore exceed the number of stored items.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Prepare a collection containing at least one item that will fail validation or persistence.
2. Import the collection.
3. Query the collection record count and its stored items.
4. Observe that the import can report success while item rows are missing.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Collection success cannot hide missing item rows.
- [ ] The selected atomicity or resumability contract is documented.
- [ ] Failed imports leave no misleading successful collection record.
- [ ] Retry behavior is deterministic and does not duplicate valid items.
- [ ] Tests cover one failed item, multiple failures, and retry after failure.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: collection imports are all-or-nothing. A failed item aborts the collection and must not leave a successful collection record with a misleading item count. Retries must be deterministic and idempotent.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Make inventory runs observe a scoped current snapshot before drift
description:
## What to build

Make an inventory-model run obtain and identify the current observation snapshot before comparing it with desired state. Drift evaluation must be scoped to the active model and workspace rather than reading every historical observation in the global catalog.

The behavior of explicit pre-ingested catalog mode must remain distinguishable from the normal live run.

## Evidence

When a model has desired resources, the run path bypasses populate. The inventory path then queries observed catalog items with an empty origin, which removes the origin filter and allows stale or unrelated observations to participate.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Import or record observations for two different models or workspaces.
2. Leave an older observation for the target model in the catalog.
3. Run the target inventory model without an explicit catalog-origin selector.
4. Observe that drift evaluation can consume stale or unrelated observations.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A normal inventory run has a defined populate or current-snapshot step before drift evaluation.
- [ ] Observations are scoped to the active model, workspace, and run or snapshot identity.
- [ ] Historical observations cannot make an unrelated run appear clean or drifted.
- [ ] Explicit catalog-backed mode has a documented and tested contract.
- [ ] Tests cover stale observations and two workspaces or models sharing one catalog.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: normal inventory runs populate live state by default, then compare against a current scoped snapshot. Catalog replay is explicit via the existing catalog-backed mode. Snapshot identity must include workspace, model, and run or snapshot identity so historical and unrelated observations cannot participate.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository

title: Honor repository workspace schema and model precedence in the CLI
description:
## What to build

Wire repository workspace context into import, schema, model, validation, and run commands so repository-local schema and model directories take precedence according to the documented workspace contract.

Global behavior must remain available when no workspace-local definitions exist.

## Evidence

Workspace discovery and origin filtering exist, but import and validation still construct their paths from the global schema configuration. The feature documentation identifies most workspace-aware CLI wiring as unfinished.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a workspace with a local schema or model that differs from the global definition.
2. Run import, schema, model, validation, and run commands from that workspace.
3. Observe which commands resolve the local definition and which continue using the global path.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Each affected command resolves local and global schema/model paths through one shared workspace policy.
- [ ] Local definitions shadow global definitions deterministically.
- [ ] Commands outside a workspace preserve current global behavior.
- [ ] Diagnostics show which workspace and paths were selected.
- [ ] Tests cover local-only, global-only, shadowed, and nested workspace cases.
- [ ] Any intentional command exceptions are documented.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.
design: Decision: workspace-local schema and model paths shadow global paths consistently across import, schema, model, validation, and run commands. Global paths remain fallback outside or when absent from a workspace. Diagnostics should show the resolved paths.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in the repository
